patterns.value: Always add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable .reveal animations + set opacity:1/transform:none. Also guard JS smooth scroll with window.matchMedia check.
:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--gold); outline-offset: 3px; } + :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } — place after all other CSS.
When hero uses position:absolute stats strip that becomes position:static on mobile, add flex-direction:column to .hero at <=768px breakpoint + width:100% on the stats element.
Always include: og:image, og:url, og:type, twitter:card, canonical link, and JSON-LD Schema.org (LegalService/Organization etc). Missing from Alpha output.
Hamburger button needs min-width:44px; min-height:44px; padding:10px and type='button' + aria-controls attribute.
Form success state used hardcoded hex #2d6a4f/#d8f3dc. Fix: use CSS variable references via btn.style.background='var(--gold-dim)'.
li::before pseudo-element combined with a .checkmark span inside the same li creates a duplicate visual checkmark. Remove li::before/after if a span handles the icon.
nav-burger as <div> is not keyboard accessible. Always use <button type='button'> with aria-expanded, aria-controls, and aria-label for hamburger menus.
outline:none on form inputs breaks keyboard navigation. Replace with outline:2px solid transparent + outline-color on :focus — keeps visual design while restoring a11y.
Animations without prefers-reduced-motion are a WCAG 2.3.3 failure. Add @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) and check JS counters/observers too.
Mobile menu overlay should be <nav> with aria-hidden toggled by JS (true when closed, false when open). Also handle Escape key to close.
ctx.roundRect() not in Safari < 16 — always wrap in typeof check with ctx.rect() fallback
Scroll-reveal animations must set opacity:1 and transform:none inside prefers-reduced-motion:reduce
Dynamic CDN scripts need both onload and onerror handlers + crossOrigin attribute
Always add -webkit-backdrop-filter before backdrop-filter for Safari support
OG tags minimum: og:type, og:url, og:title, og:description, og:image + twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image
cursor:none on body breaks touch devices and low-vision users — wrap in @media (pointer:fine) and mirror in JS with matchMedia check
#52525C on dark backgrounds fails WCAG AA (2.6:1). Minimum accessible muted on #06060A is #7A7A88 (4.6:1). Always verify contrast before using muted palette tokens.

Hidden nav ul with display:none on mobile without a burger toggle JS is a critical mobile navigation failure — always pair with toggle button + aria-expanded + close-on-link-click + Escape key handler

Horror/atmospheric sites use heavy animations (grain, fog, glitch, flicker). Always add @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce) block + JS check before registering IntersectionObserver and setInterval effects
Footer/CTA links that look like placeholders (href='#') should be given real URLs or clearly marked. All real external links need target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'
Always add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable all animations. Also set opacity:1 on elements that rely on animation to become visible, or they stay invisible for reduced-motion users.
min-height: 100svh requires fallback: min-height: 100vh first for Safari < 15.4 and older browsers
rgba color with low alpha (0.45-0.6) on dark or light backgrounds often fails WCAG 4.5:1 contrast. Bump to 0.75-0.85 minimum. Use WebAIM checker on blended hex value.
Mobile hamburger button must have aria-expanded='false' initially and toggle to 'true' on open. Also update aria-label to 'Закрыть меню' when open. Add Escape key handler to close.
Skip-to-content link: position absolute, top -100%, show on :focus. Targets #main-content which wraps all sections inside <main>. Required for WCAG 2.4.1.
All pages lacked meta description and OG tags — always check head section first in QA
Nav with 60px padding + 3-4 col grids break at 390px — add breakpoints at 768px and 480px for all multi-column grids
All interactive buttons lacked type='button' — causes accidental form submission; always add type to every <button>
Pages with CSS animations had no prefers-reduced-motion fallback — add @media block to every animated page
Consultation form inputs had <label> without for/id pairs — link all labels to inputs for WCAG AA compliance
Pattern: hide nav-links at 768px, show nav-burger, fullscreen overlay nav-mobile with close button — works on all forte subpages
